title: Plaid Power: National Tartan Day
story: Bagpipes echo through the streets as people proudly wear their colorful plaid patterns on National Tartan Day every April 6! This Scottish celebration honors the beautiful woven designs called tartans that represent different families and clans. People show off kilts, scarves, and ties in bold checkered patterns while enjoying Scottish music and dancing.
activity_question: What are the colorful plaid patterns called that represent Scottish families?
follow_up_question: What tartan pattern would you design for your own family?

title: Sports Unite the World Day
story: Every April 6, countries around the globe celebrate the International Day of Sport for Development and Peace! Schools organize friendly soccer matches between rival classes, communities host basketball tournaments that bring neighbors together, and athletes share messages about teamwork on social media. The day shows how sports can build friendships across different cultures and solve problems peacefully through play instead of conflict.
activity_question: What do schools often organize on this holiday?
activity_choices: Friendly soccer matches between rival classes
activity_answer: Friendly soccer matches between rival classes
follow_up_question: How do you think sports help people from different countries become friends?
